text: Espousals. He speaks ironically. (Theodoret)<hr>Yet the Israelites at first shewed greater proofs of love than they did afterwards. It is true they often prevaricated, in the wilderness, Exodus 32:1., and Amos 5:25., and Psalm lxxvii., etc. (Calmet)<hr>The Lord declares his gratuitous love, and then upbraids his people. (Haydock)<hr>He had caused them to multiply in Egypt, and gratuitously made choice of them. (Worthington)
Increase; most desirable, Osee 9:10. God punished those who attempted to injure his people: yet they abandoned his service.
Vanity; idols, whom he will not mention, to spare their shame. (Calmet)
Carmel. That is, a fruitful, plentiful land. (Challoner) (Worthington)<hr>All Palestine is thus designated. (Menochius)

Priests were silent, or abandoned themselves.<hr>Pastors; "princes." (Chaldean) Manasses, Amon, etc.<hr>In Baal, promoting his worship. (Haydock)<hr>The land was full of false prophets, and none stood up for the Lord.
Children; the Israelites, under Moses, and their posterity transgressed.
Cethim; Macedon. It here denotes the western nations, as Cedar does those on the east.
Glory; the true God and his ark, Psalm 3:4., and 1 Kings 4:21.
Gates; angels, or the temple.
Water. The idols and nations, to which they have had recourse, injure them. (Calmet)<hr>The Jews did just the reverse to what God commanded. (Worthington)
Slave. From such, labour and tribute were required, Matthew 17:24., and John 8:33.
Lions; kings of Assyria, etc. The decree for the devastation of Juda had already passed, though it was not put in execution.
Taphnes, 16 miles from Pelusium, in Egypt. The nation proved only detrimental to the Jews, by engaging them in their abominations, instead of affording relief.

Troubled. Hebrew shichor, or Nile water, (Josue 13:3.) which was thought as good as wine, and grew better for keeping. (Strabo xvii.)<hr>Hence the people adored it. (Vitruvius viii.)<hr>God often reproached the Jews for distrusting in his protection, and seeking aid from the Egyptians, who deceived them. We know not that Josias did so. (Calmet)<hr>He even opposed them, and lost his life in defending the country, 4 Kings 23:29. (Haydock)<hr>He was probably obliged to pay tribute to the Assyrians, whom Achaz had called in, 2 Paralipomenon 28:23. These alliances the Lord condemned.
Thou. Septuagint. Yet Hebrew and Chaldean have, "I have permissively broken," or foretold this infidelity.
Chosen. Hebrew, Sorek, Judges 16:4., and Isaias 5:2., and 16:8. (Calmet)<hr>God created all things good, planted his Church in justice, and no evil proceeds from Him. (Worthington)
Borith. An herb used to clean clothes, and take out spots and dirt, (Challoner) like kali, soda, (Calmet) or soap. (Langius.)<hr>Protestants, "and take thee much soap." (Haydock)

Valley of Hinnom, under the very walls of Jerusalem. (Calmet)<hr>Runner. Hebrew, "dromedary," which takes its name from its swiftness. (Haydock)<hr>The female camel continues all day with the male, and cannot be approached. (Aristotle; Pliny, [Natural History?] 10:63.)<hr>Juda is represented as no less libidinous. (Calmet)
Away. The female may easily be found by the poisonous hippo-manes. (Georg. iii.)<hr>Thus Jerusalem is an impudent prostitute. (Haydock)
Foot, and the parts which modesty covers. My exhortations are slighted. (Calmet)<hr>Despair opens the door to every sort of impurity, Ephesians 4:19. (Haydock)
Taken. Juda affected still to be very pious, ver. 23.
Cities. All were abandoned, Ezechiel 16:24., and Osee 10:1.
Prophets; Zacharias, (2 Paralipomenon 24:21.) Isaias, etc., Matthew 23:34. (Calmet)<hr>Punishment is designed by God to cause people to repent. (Worthington)
See, or hear. The sword seems to be animated. (Calmet)<hr>Lateward. Hebrew, "darksome land." Have I not heaped blessings on my people?<hr>Revolted. Protestants, "Lords." (Haydock)
Thou who. Hebrew, "Therefore have I." (Calmet)<hr>Protestants, "hast thou also taught the wicked ones thy ways." (Haydock)<hr>Thou hast opened a school of vice.
Innocent children, immolated to Moloc, or people murdered, whose blood thou hast not concealed, 4 Kings 21:16.
Head, like the violated Thamar, 2 Kings 13:19. The king of Egypt was routed, when coming to assist Sedecias, Jeremias 37:3, 10. (Calmet)
